//: GitHub Projects v2 example — copy this dir's files into your project, rename to agenthook.config.json (or run `agenthook init`), then fill the YOUR_* placeholders. See README.md here and docs/github-projects-setup.md.
//secrets: Secrets are ${ENV} refs resolved from the shell or a .env beside this file, so this file stays shareable. State lives centrally in ~/.agenthook/<name>.
//board: Create the board Status options FIRST — Projects v2 Status options are NOT auto-created. Add every option named below (Triage, In Progress, In Review, Done, Blocked, Needs Info) to the board's Status field (Project → ⋯ → Settings → Status) before starting.
name: github-projects-example
//fullAuto: false (safe default) = agents prompt for permission. true = --dangerously-skip-permissions: a verified webhook runs UNSANDBOXED code on the host. Opt in only on a trusted host or in a container. See docs/architecture.md#security-posture.

tracker.//: github-projects routes on a Projects v2 board's Status single-select field. Steps bind Status OPTION NAMES (sourceStatus/successStatus/failureStatus/holdStatus), matched case-insensitively — same keys as Jira.
tracker.//project: owner/number — an ORG and the project number (or paste the project URL). Use an ORG-owned project so the projects_v2_item webhook can auto-create with admin:org_hook; a user-owned project has no PAT webhook path. The assignee login for scoping is read from the token's viewer.
tracker.//repository: owner/name — only used by `agenthook run` to add a loose issue to the board; omit if you set a card's Status on the board by hand.
tracker.//pipeline: The execution model (required). A card whose Status ENTERS a step's sourceStatus fires that step. On exit the agent writes a VERDICT and the receiver SETS the card's Status (single-occupancy — one mutation, a card is in exactly one stage):
tracker.//pipeline2: advance -> successStatus (= the next step's source, so the move triggers it); fail -> failureStatus (a human picks it up); hold -> holdStatus (parked for a human answer); changes -> a prior step's source (the rework loop, capped by maxAttempts, default 3).
tracker.//pipeline3: createsWorktree makes the receiver own a git worktree shared across the card's steps; drainWorktree removes it; manual = no agent (entry only runs system actions). Each step's instructionsFile is its standing prompt (relative to this file).

pipeline.//maxAttempts: cap on runs per card before a `changes` loop into this step fails (default 3)

pipeline.//changes: a review `changes` verdict bounces back to the previous step (code) on the same branch/PR

pipeline.//done: terminal: a card reaching Done runs no agent — it only drains the receiver-owned worktree the code step created.

ingress.//: STABLE ingress REQUIRED — the projects_v2_item webhook is ORG-scoped at a FIXED URL, so an ephemeral tunnel would orphan the org hook each boot (same constraint as Jira). Use a reserved ngrok domain or a hosted URL.
ingress.//hosted: Or a hosted static URL instead: { "type": "hosted", "url": "https://hook.you.com" }
